title_en: Introduction to Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq by Ibn Nujaym
title_ar: مقدمة في البحر الرائق لابن نجيم
content_en:
Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq Sharh Kanz ad-Daqa'iq ('The Flowing Ocean: Commentary on the Treasure of Subtleties') is the most important Hanafi legal commentary of the Ottoman period and a cornerstone reference of the Hanafi school to this day. Its author, Zayn ad-Din Ibrahim ibn Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Ibn Nujaym al-Misri al-Hanafi (d. 970 AH / 1563 CE), is considered one of the foremost Hanafi jurists of the tenth century AH and is known as 'the ash-Shafi'i of the Hanafi school' for the depth and systematic quality of his legal reasoning.

Ibn Nujaym composed Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq as a commentary on Kanz ad-Daqa'iq, the celebrated concise Hanafi legal text by Hafiz ad-Din an-Nasafi (d. 710 AH). Where Kanz ad-Daqa'iq presents the rulings in condensed form, Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq expands each ruling with its evidential basis, the reasoning of the foundational Hanafi scholars (Abu Hanifa, Abu Yusuf, Muhammad ash-Shaybani), the positions of other schools where relevant, and Ibn Nujaym's own careful analysis of disputed questions.

Ibn Nujaym's scholarly output beyond Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq includes Al-Ashbah wan-Naza'ir (the Hanafi equivalent of as-Suyuti's work of the same title on legal maxims), his commentary on al-Kanz for Hanafi usul al-fiqh, and numerous fatwas. His Al-Ashbah wan-Naza'ir became the standard Hanafi reference on legal maxims, enshrining the 'five universal maxims' and hundreds of additional Hanafi-specific principles.

Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq was completed by Ibn Nujaym up to the chapter on divorce (talaq), after which it was completed by his student Muhammad ibn Husayn al-Tumurtashi. The printed editions include the completion alongside Ibn Nujaym's own text, and later scholars such as Muhammad Amin ibn 'Abidin (author of Radd al-Muhtar) added extensive marginal notes.

The title 'The Flowing Ocean' was chosen by Ibn Nujaym to convey the comprehensive and flowing nature of the work — it aspires to cover every significant question in Hanafi law as completely as an ocean covers the sea floor, leaving no significant area unexplored. This aspiration was largely realized: Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq is consistently cited in Hanafi scholarship whenever a comprehensive treatment of a topic is needed.

Studying Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq provides access to the full depth of Hanafi legal reasoning in its late-classical period, representing the culmination of the school's Egyptian tradition and its engagement with both the core transmitted texts and the challenges of the Ottoman-era Muslim world.
title_id: Pengantar Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq oleh Ibn Nujaym
content_id:
Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq Sharh Kanz ad-Daqa'iq ("Samudra Mengalir: Komentar atas Perbendaharaan Hal-Hal Halus") merupakan komentar hukum Hanafi terpenting pada masa Utsmaniyah dan rujukan utama mazhab Hanafi hingga hari ini. Pengarangnya, Zayn ad-Din Ibrahim ibn Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Ibn Nujaym al-Misri al-Hanafi (w. 970 H / 1563 M), dianggap sebagai salah satu ahli fiqih Hanafi terkemuka pada abad kesepuluh Hijriah dan dijuluki "asy-Syafi'i-nya mazhab Hanafi" karena kedalaman dan kualitas pemikiran hukumnya yang sistematis.

Ibn Nujaym menyusun Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq sebagai komentar atas Kanz ad-Daqa'iq, teks hukum Hanafi ringkas yang terkenal karya Hafiz ad-Din an-Nasafi (w. 710 H). Jika Kanz ad-Daqa'iq menyajikan hukum-hukum secara padat, maka Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq menguraikan setiap hukum berikut landasan dalilnya, pemikiran para ulama pendiri Hanafi (Abu Hanifah, Abu Yusuf, Muhammad asy-Syaibani), posisi mazhab lain bila relevan, serta analisis mendalam Ibn Nujaym sendiri atas persoalan-persoalan yang diperdebatkan.

Karya ilmiah Ibn Nujaym selain Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq mencakup Al-Asybah wan-Naza'ir (padanan Hanafi dari karya as-Suyuthi dengan judul sama tentang kaidah-kaidah hukum), komentarnya atas al-Kanz untuk usul fiqih Hanafi, dan berbagai fatwa. Karya Al-Asybah wan-Naza'ir-nya menjadi rujukan standar Hanafi dalam kaidah hukum, merumuskan "lima kaidah universal" dan ratusan prinsip khusus Hanafi lainnya.

Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq diselesaikan Ibn Nujaym hingga bab talak, kemudian dilanjutkan oleh muridnya Muhammad ibn Husain al-Tumurtasyi. Edisi-edisi cetak mencantumkan bagian pelengkap bersama teks Ibn Nujaym sendiri, dan para ulama kemudian seperti Muhammad Amin ibn 'Abidin (pengarang Radd al-Muhtar) menambahkan catatan-catatan pinggir yang luas.

Judul "Samudra Mengalir" dipilih Ibn Nujaym untuk mencerminkan sifat karya yang komprehensif dan mengalir — ia bermaksud mencakup setiap persoalan penting dalam hukum Hanafi selengkap samudra menyelimuti dasar laut, tanpa meninggalkan satu pun bidang penting yang tak dibahas. Ambisi ini sebagian besar terwujud: Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq senantiasa dikutip dalam khazanah Hanafi setiap kali diperlukan pembahasan komprehensif tentang suatu topik.

Mempelajari Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq membuka akses ke kedalaman penuh penalaran hukum Hanafi pada periode akhir klasiknya, yang merepresentasikan puncak tradisi Mesir mazhab tersebut dan keterlibatannya dengan teks-teks inti yang ditransmisikan maupun tantangan-tantangan dunia Muslim era Utsmaniyah.
